perturbation.original_text: A. "Confidential Information" of a party shall mean any trade secrets, know-how, inventions, products, designs, methods, techniques, systems, processes, software programs, works of authorship, business plans, customer lists, projects, plans, pricing, proposals and any other information which a party discloses to the Recipient Party that: (i) if disclosed in writing is clearly marked as confidential or carries a similar legend; or (ii) if disclosed verbally or in tangible form is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, then summarized in a writing so marked by the Disclosing Party and delivered to the Recipient Party with fifteen (15) days. Confidential Information shall also include the terms of this agreement, the fact that the information is being made available to the parties hereunder and the fact that the parties are contemplating a possible business arrangement.
perturbation.changed_text: A. "Confidential Information" of a party shall mean any trade secrets, know-how, inventions, products, designs, methods, techniques, systems, processes, software programs, works of authorship, business plans, customer lists, projects, plans, pricing, proposals and any other information which a party discloses to the Recipient Party that: (i) if disclosed in writing is clearly marked as confidential or carries a similar legend; or (ii) if disclosed verbally or in tangible form is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure. Confidential Information shall not include the terms of this agreement, the fact that the information is being made available to the parties hereunder and the fact that the parties are contemplating a possible business arrangement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that explicitly includes 'the terms of this agreement' as Confidential Information, it creates a conflict. Other clauses within the agreement rely on this definition to enforce confidentiality. This introduces ambiguity, as the recipient party might argue that the agreement's terms are not protected under the confidentiality obligations. This modification impacts the enforcement of confidentiality concerning the agreement's existence and terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. "Confidential Information" of a party shall mean any trade secrets, know-how, inventions, products, designs, methods, techniques, systems, processes, software programs, works of authorship, business plans, customer lists, projects, plans, pricing, proposals and any other information which a party discloses to the Recipient Party that: (i) if disclosed in writing is clearly marked as confidential or carries a similar legend; or (ii) if disclosed verbally or in tangible form is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure. Confidential Information shall not include the terms of this agreement, the fact that the information is being made available to the parties hereunder and the fact that the parties are contemplating a possible business arrangement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit inclusion of 'the terms of this agreement' from the definition of Confidential Information. The contradicted text relies on this definition to enforce confidentiality. Because the change alters the definition, and the contradicted text references that definition, the change creates a direct contradiction, as the terms of the agreement may now be interpreted as not confidential, impacting enforcement. This adheres to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, creating a conflicting obligation.
